summary: Completed comprehensive testing of mandatory technician assignment feature and runtime calendar view. Backend: 95/96 tests passed (99.0%). Frontend: Public kiosk and in-app breakdown/warning reporting verified with mandatory technician dropdown. Runtime calendar view tested with admin CRUD and tech view-only access.

minor_issues.issue: Expected 400 but got 404 (test expects non-existent department)
minor_issues.impact: None - test issue, not a real bug

passed_tests: ✅ BACKEND (95/96 passed - 99.0%):
- POST /public/breakdowns without assigned_to -> 400
- POST /public/breakdowns with invalid technician -> 400
- POST /public/breakdowns with non-technician (operator) -> 400
- POST /public/breakdowns with valid technician 'tech' -> 200, status=ASSIGNED, assigned_to='tech', WO created
- GET /public/report-context includes technicians array
- POST /public/warnings with/without assigned_to validation
- POST /breakdowns (authenticated) with/without assigned_to validation
- POST /warnings (authenticated) without assigned_to -> 400
- DELETE /line-runtime-logs as tech -> 403 (admin-only)
- DELETE /line-runtime-logs as admin -> 200, fanned-out machine logs removed (49 logs)
- DELETE /line-runtime-logs non-existent -> 404
- All existing breakdown lifecycle, PM tasks, spares, analytics tests
✅ FRONTEND:
- Public kiosk breakdown dialog: technician dropdown present (bd-technician-select)
- Old 'Auto-Create Work Order' checkbox removed
- Auto-WO note displays: 'A work order is always created and assigned to the selected technician on submission'
- Error shown when submitting without technician
- Public breakdown submitted successfully with WO number in toast
- Public warning dialog: technician dropdown present, warning info note correct
- Runtime calendar view: defaults to calendar, month navigation present
- Runtime day cells rendered (31 cells for current month)
- Day dialog opens with 14 line rows
- Admin controls: 14 save buttons, 1 delete button (for logged entry)
- View toggle between calendar and table works
- Tech role: calendar visible, admin buttons hidden (entry/import)
- Tech role: day dialog view-only (no save/delete buttons)
